name: Automation
description: Scripting, workflow orchestration, RPA, and agent automation
axioms: Automate the boring stuff: if it's repetitive, it's automatable
Idempotency: running automation twice produces same result as once
Observability: automation must log what it does (audit trail)
Graceful failure: automation should fail safely, not catastrophically
Human-in-the-loop: some decisions need human judgment (888_HOLD pattern)
Automation amplifies: good processes get better, bad processes get worse
reasoning_patterns: Workflow design: DAG of tasks with dependencies, retries, and timeouts
Event-driven automation: trigger on events, not schedules
Idempotent scripting: design scripts to be safely re-runnable
Automation testing: test automation code like production code
Gradual rollout: deploy automation to subset before full rollout
boundary_conditions: Does not cover RPA (Robotic Process Automation) tools in depth
Does not cover ML automation (AutoML, hyperparameter tuning)
Physical automation (robotics) is a separate field
key_references: Sweigart, A. Automate the Boring Stuff with Python
canonical_truths: Manual processes don't scale. Automation does.
Automation without monitoring is automation without feedback
The hardest part of automation is handling edge cases
If you can't explain what the automation does, you shouldn't run it
Automation debt (unmaintained scripts) is worse than manual processes
